explanation.ask_line: You are being asked to share a verification code, OTP, or one-time password.
explanation.tactic_line: Scammers use codes to take over your accounts or approve unauthorized transactions.
explanation.outcome_line: Once they have the code, they can empty your account or lock you out.
explanation.suggestion_line: Never share codes with anyone. Real companies will never ask for them by text or email.

explanation.ask_line: You are being asked to transfer money, make a deposit, or send funds.
explanation.tactic_line: Scammers create urgency so you act before you can verify the request.
explanation.outcome_line: Money sent to scammers is usually impossible to recover.
explanation.suggestion_line: Verify any payment request through a known channel. Do not pay or transfer based on messages alone.

explanation.ask_line: You are being asked to grant access, follow an order from authority, or install software so someone can control your device.
explanation.tactic_line: Scammers pretend to be your boss or IT to get you to bypass normal security.
explanation.outcome_line: They can steal files, lock your computer, or use your access to steal more.
explanation.suggestion_line: Confirm requests through official channels. Do not install remote-access software or share access based on a message.

explanation.ask_line: You are being asked to click a link, often to verify an account or view something.
explanation.tactic_line: Scammers use links to steal passwords or install malware.
explanation.outcome_line: The site may look real but capture your login or infect your device.
explanation.suggestion_line: Type the company URL yourself or use a bookmark. Do not click links in unexpected messages.

explanation.ask_line: You are being asked to download or run a file or attachment.
explanation.tactic_line: Scammers use files to install malware that steals data or locks your device.
explanation.outcome_line: Opening the file can give them full control of your computer.
explanation.suggestion_line: Do not open attachments or downloads from unexpected senders. Verify with the sender through another channel.

explanation.ask_line: You are committing to an action or availability.
explanation.tactic_line: People often agree quickly in conversations without checking conflicts.
explanation.outcome_line: This can cause missed obligations or reputational damage.
explanation.suggestion_line: Verify your schedule or ability before confirming.

explanation.ask_line: This message does not clearly ask you to do a specific risky action.
explanation.tactic_line: Many scams rely on urgency, fake authority, or emotional pressure.
explanation.outcome_line: Staying cautious with unexpected messages helps avoid harm.
explanation.suggestion_line: If something feels off, verify through a known contact or official channel before acting.
